citations.summary: Linear covariance-root change of variables moves the localized fluctuation integral to the product standard Gaussian interface.
use_for: Source anchor for Gaussian pushforward after the covariance-root change of variables.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat this as the dictionary coordinate identification, determinant normalization, or root-localization theorem.
open_questions: Exact coordinate/dictionary statement for the repository's physical fluctuation field.
Normalization/Jacobian assumptions needed by the Lean Gaussian pushforward field.

citations.summary: Balaban localizes the product-Gaussian expression into quantities H(Z,Z0) and H(Z), with component factorization.
use_for: Source anchor for existence of localized activity H(Z) before Lemma 3 estimates.
do_not_use_for: Do not claim exact finite covariance-root reconstruction from this citation.
Do not claim the repository's active support inclusion without the enlargement convention.
open_questions: Exact map from Balaban localization domains to OmegaPolymerType supports.
Exact active-support and external-field locality theorem needed by Appendix-F support hypotheses.

citations.summary: Main resummation window for the activity H(Z), ending in Lemma 3 and the final exponential activity estimate.
use_for: Navigation to the exact source region for the remaining Lemma 3 boundary discharges.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite the OCR text as exact formula data.
Do not use this broad window as a single monolithic Lean hypothesis.
open_questions: Exact source-to-Lean dictionary for the H(Z) termwise summation (2.14) and the D/P/Z0/Z0' dependent indices.
External Cammarota theorem and threshold dependencies needed to theorem-feed the visually confirmed Eq. (2.29) D-stage display.
Exact Eq. (2.31) P-family carrier, injection, and carrier-count theorem.
Formal constant-hierarchy theorem turning the visually confirmed Eq. (2.37), alpha5/C3 region, and smallness assumptions into repository postPSourceWeight/postPAmplitude fields.

citations.summary: Visually confirmed D-stage summability after the product estimate following (2.28): for K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small, the D-family product sum is at most one.
extracted_claims: Before Eq. (2.27), Balaban fixes one connected component Y0 and considers the product over Y in D in Eq. (2.26), extracting alpha6 * exp(-delta * kappa * d_k(Y)) from each factor.
Eq. (2.27) states sum_{Y in D} (d_k(Y) + 5) >= d_k(Y0) + 5, using that union_{Y in D} Y = Y0 and Y0 is connected.

Under the displayed smallness condition 2*E0*epsilon1*C1*alpha4^-1*alpha6^-1*M^q*exp(C2*kappa1)*exp(5*kappa) <= 1, Eq. (2.28) bounds the product from Eq. (2.26) by the Eq. (2.29) product times the remaining Y0 exponential prefactor.
Eq. (2.29) states sum_D product_{Y in D} alpha6 * exp(-delta * kappa * d_k(Y)) <= 1 for K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small.
Eq. (2.30) records the domain metric comparison (3*2^3)^-1 * M^-4 * |Y| <= d_k(Y) <= M^-4 * |Y| - 1 for localization domains Y in D_k.
use_for: Source anchor for the Eq. (2.29) D-family product, the product-sum bound, and the adjacent Eq. (2.27)/(2.30) metric comparisons.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat the qualitative phrase 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' as a formal uniform threshold without extracting or proving the cited combinatorial argument.
Do not identify the repository's DIndex/DParts with Balaban's D-families until the source-to-Lean dictionary is supplied.
open_questions: Exact source-to-Lean dictionary for DIndex, DParts, and the localization-domain metric d_k.
Formal proof or source theorem for the large-K/small-alpha6 summability assertion, including threshold dependencies and uniformity over scale, volume, and background.
Exact role of the cited argument in [26] used to prove inequalities of this type.

citations.summary: Visual identification of the reference [26] cited by CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type summability as C. Cammarota, Decay of correlations for infinite range interactions in unbounded spin systems, CMP 85, 517-528 (1982).
extracted_claims: CMP109 printed page 299, reference 26, lists C. Cammarota: Decay of correlations for infinite range interactions in unbounded spin systems, Communications in Mathematical Physics 85, 517-528 (1982).
CMP116 printed page 18 says inequalities of the Eq. (2.29) type can be proved by a simple modification of the argument in [26].
This citation identifies the external source to inspect next; it does not extract Cammarota's theorem, constants, hypotheses, or its translation to Balaban D-families.
use_for: Bibliographic source lookup for the external combinatorial/cluster-expansion argument behind CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type product summability.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as a theorem proving Eq. (2.29).
Do not infer threshold dependencies for K or alpha6 from this bibliographic identification alone.
Do not identify Cammarota's polymer/spin-system hypotheses with the repository's DIndex/DParts without extracting the actual theorem.
open_questions: Exact theorem, lemma, proposition, or equation in Cammarota CMP85 proving the polymer-family summability argument needed by Balaban.
Constants and threshold dependencies needed to convert Cammarota's argument into the qualitative CMP116 condition K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small.
Precise dictionary from Cammarota's interaction/polymer objects to Balaban's D-families and then to the repository's DIndex/DParts fields.

citations.summary: Primary bibliographic page located for Cammarota CMP85, the external argument CMP116 cites for Eq. (2.29)-type summability; the exact theorem, constants, hypotheses, and Balaban D-family dictionary are still not extracted.
extracted_claims: Springer confirms the article metadata: C. Cammarota, Communications in Mathematical Physics 85, 517-528 (1982), DOI 10.1007/BF01403502.
The Springer abstract says the paper uses an associated polymer model and gives a proof of convergence of the Mayer series for the general polymer model.
Project Euclid advertises a PDF endpoint for this article, but the automation environment received an anti-bot HTML page rather than a PDF.
ResearchGate exposes an author-uploaded OCR/full-text view, but it is not yet a local primary PDF artifact and its OCR is too corrupted to theorem-feed Eq. (2.29).
use_for: Precise source request and access ledger for the Cammarota theorem behind CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type D-family summability.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as a theorem proving Eq. (2.29).
Do not use ResearchGate OCR to set Lean constants or thresholds.
Do not identify Cammarota polymers with Balaban D-families or repository DIndex/DParts until the exact theorem and dictionary are extracted.
open_questions: Exact printed theorem/lemma/proposition/equation in Cammarota CMP85 that proves the polymer-family summability used by Balaban.
Exact smallness hypothesis, constants, metric/diameter convention, dimension dependence, and uniformity in volume.
Exact route from Cammarota's polymer-model theorem to Balaban's qualitative large-K/small-alpha6 Eq. (2.29) assertion.
Exact source-to-Lean dictionary from Cammarota polymers to Balaban D-families and then to DIndex/DParts.

citations.summary: Visually confirmed Eq. (2.31) P-summation with fixed Z0 and Y0, controlled by the exponential factor involving the finite bond set P.
extracted_claims: For fixed Z0 and Y0, the displayed P-sum has factors exp(-rho * M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0|) and exp(-2 * rho * |P|), where rho = (1/20) * gamma2 * epsilon1^2 / gk^2.
The displayed powerset/cardinality bound is exp(-M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0| * (rho - 4 * M^4 * exp(-2 * rho))) <= 1.
The localization/counting scale visible in Eq. (2.31) is M, and the Lean gapMass abstraction corresponds to M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0|.
The source text immediately before Eq. (2.31) describes P as a set of bonds and says |P| >= (1/2) * M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0| because one bond in P may connect two cubes in Z0 \ Y0.
use_for: Source anchor for the Eq. (2.31) rate rho, gapMass shape, exact bracket condition rho - 4*M^4*exp(-2*rho), and finite bond-subset entropy bound.
do_not_use_for: Do not assert the repository's pBonds_injective, pBonds_subset, or PIndex dictionary until the exact source-to-Lean set map is supplied.
Do not cite the stronger Lean sufficient condition 80*M^4*gk^2 <= gamma2*epsilon1^2 as a verbatim source hypothesis.
open_questions: Exact dictionary from Balaban's finite bond set P to the repository's PIndex/pBonds fields.
Formal source proof of the carrier/cardinality bound used as bondCarrier_card_le in Lean.
Exact parameter-hierarchy statement relating gk, gamma, gamma2, epsilon1, M, and kappa after Eq. (2.31).

citations.summary: Source-targeting entry for the exact Balaban P-family membership theorem and eligible bond carrier theorem needed before identifying source PIndex with the filtered Lean family.
extracted_claims: The visually confirmed Eq. (2.31) display fixes the rate rho, the gap factor M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0|, and the powerset/cardinality bracket used by the current finite-sum theorem.
The visually confirmed CMP116 page 12 formula defines Y0^{c,*} as bonds b in T^(k) contained in Y0^c, excluding b0(c) for c in T^(k+1); Eq. (2.3) sums over P subsets of this bond set.
CMP116 page 12 takes Z0 as the smallest localization domain containing Y0 and P, and requires bonds of P to be in the interior of Z0 and not intersect dZ0.
The visually confirmed paragraph describes P as a finite bond set and gives the lower bound |P| >= (1/2)*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0|.

The inspected citation material does not yet give a formal source-to-Lean map from Balaban's P to repository PIndex/pBonds, nor does it prove the carrier upper bound recorded by CMP116Eq231PBondBoundary.
Lean now has a source-neutral extensional bridge: a pointwise membership iff for PIndex yields equality with cmp116Eq231SourcePIndex, but the source membership iff remains pending.
use_for: Target for proving the pointwise membership iff that identifies Balaban's source P-family with the filtered Lean PIndex family.
Precise source request for removing the live carrier-containment hypothesis from concrete source-facing P-stage and M3 routes.
Checklist for the source facts required to justify the four-positive-direction carrier used by the concrete finite-bond-set PIndex route.
do_not_use_for: Do not construct a source-native PIndex, pBonds_injective, pBonds_subset, gapMass, or bondCarrier_card_le theorem from this entry alone.
Do not treat the source lower bound on |P| as the carrier upper bound needed by Lean.
Do not replace CMP116Eq231PBondBoundary with a record that merely repackages the same arbitrary data under a source name.
open_questions: Exact pointwise membership characterization: P is in Balaban's source P-family iff P is contained in the eligible carrier and satisfies the admissibility condition used by cmp116Eq231SourcePIndex.
Exact definition of the eligible bond universe for fixed Z0 and Y0, including orientation and adjacency conventions.
Whether Balaban's P is literally a subset of that eligible carrier or an encoded object requiring a quotient/dictionary.
Proof that the source P-family has an injective representation as finite bond subsets in the repository convention.
Proof of the carrier count |Carrier(Z0,Y0)| <= 4*|Z0 \ Y0|, equivalently <= 4*M^4*gapMass under the Lean gapMass convention.
Exact relationship between the source lower bound |P| >= (1/2)*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0| and the repository weight exp(-2*rho*|P|).

citations.summary: Candidate CMP109 bond-convention source windows: subsets determine bonds as nearest-neighbor intervals, bonds have endpoints b_- and b_+, and later CMP109 formulas restrict b to positively oriented bonds.
extracted_claims: CMP109 printed page 251 says a continuous-space subset determines lattice points, bonds, and plaquettes, and describes bonds as intervals connecting nearest-neighbor points that intersect the subset.
CMP109 printed page 262 writes a bond as b = (b_-, b_+) when defining endpoint-dependent gauge transformations.
CMP109 printed page 269 says that in the displayed transformation formulas the bonds b are positively oriented, and recalls that the relevant representation formula holds for such bonds only.

These windows support the search for a four-positive-direction carrier convention, but they do not by themselves identify the CMP116 Eq. (2.31) P-family carrier or prove the filtered PIndex membership iff.
use_for: Companion source target for the oriented-bond convention needed by the CMP116 Eq. (2.31) filtered P-family dictionary.
Input for a precise source/pro-advisor request asking for the missing bridge from CMP109's general bond convention to CMP116's fixed-(Z0,Y0) eligible P carrier.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as proving that Balaban's Eq. (2.31) P-family equals cmp116Eq231SourcePIndex.
Do not infer the carrier bound |Carrier(Z0,Y0)| <= 4*|Z0 \ Y0| from these windows alone.
Do not treat positive orientation here as the complete source-to-Lean dictionary for Cube x Fin 4.
open_questions: Exact CMP116 or surrounding-source statement that the P-family near Eq. (2.31) uses the same positively oriented bond convention.
Exact eligible carrier for fixed (Z0,Y0) and proof that every source P is a subset of it.
Exact carrier cardinality theorem and the source-to-Lean conversion to gapCubes Z D x Finset.univ.

citations.summary: CMP109 definition of the special fine-lattice bond b0(c) via the corridor B(c), used to decode the CMP116 page-12 exclusion in Y0^{c,*}.
extracted_claims: CMP109 printed page 267 defines an operator h from bond fields on T(k+1) to bond fields on T(k), with hB supported only on the special bonds b0(c).
For c in T(k+1), b0(c) is the bond of the lattice T(k) contained in c and belonging to the corridor B(c), whose endpoints lie in the endpoint blocks B(c_-) and B(c_+).

This visually confirmed window explains the b0(c) notation excluded from CMP116 page 12's Y0^{c,*} set, but it still does not identify the fixed-(Z0,Y0) carrier or prove the four-direction cardinality bound.
use_for: Source anchor for interpreting the b0(c) exclusion in the CMP116 Eq. (2.3) P-family setup.
Input for the precise source/pro-advisor request asking whether Y0^{c,*} can be converted into the repository's four-positive-direction carrier.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as proving the Eq. (2.31) filtered PIndex membership iff.
Do not use the b0(c) corridor definition alone to prove |Carrier(Z0,Y0)| <= 4*|Z0 \ Y0|.
Do not treat h's support set as the complete CMP116 P-family carrier; CMP116 uses the complement after removing the b0(c) bonds.
open_questions: How CMP116's Y0^{c,*} and the later smallest-domain resummation over Z0 translate into the fixed-(Z0,Y0) eligible carrier.
Whether the b0(c) exclusion interacts with the repository's carrier gapCubes Z D x Finset.univ or is already absorbed by admissible Z D P.
Exact source or elementary finite-lattice proof of the carrier count after the b0(c) bonds are removed.

citations.summary: Post-P resummation over D, P, and Z0 families leading to the fixed Z0' bound before the final Z0' summation.
extracted_claims: The paragraph before Eq. (2.37) estimates sums over connected components Z_i' of a fixed Z0' by using (1.28), then Eq. (2.29), with the smallness assumptions (L+2)^4*O(1)*epsilon2 <= 1/2 and 2*(L+2)^4*O(1)*epsilon2*exp(5*kappa) <= 1.

Eq. (2.37) bounds the partially resummed absolute value for fixed Z0' by exp(-(kappa1 - 1)*(LM)^-4*|Z \ Z0'|) times a product over connected components Z_i' of Z0' of 2*(L+2)^4*O(1)*epsilon2*exp(-((1 - 7*delta)/2)*L*kappa*d_{k+1}(Z_i')), multiplied by exp(O(1)*alpha5*|Z|).

The paragraph following Eq. (2.37) performs the last sum over Z0' or Z \ Z0'. It uses the adapted Eq. (2.32), half of the first exponential factor, the assumption (1/2)*(kappa1 - 1) >= 2*L*kappa, and an Eq. (2.34)-type estimate.

This supports the Lean design in which Eq. (2.37) plus the following paragraph feed a combined post-P source boundary and a separate majorization into the canonical Lemma-3 base factor; it does not justify separate normalized Z0 and Z0' source theorems by itself.
use_for: Source anchor for the combined post-P source boundary supplied to CMP116PostPResidualSourceBound.
Source anchor for the exponent-reserve step that turns the page-20 post-P weight into the canonical Lemma-3 decay weight once the residual exponent is explicitly bounded.
Source anchor for treating the post-P stage as order-sensitive and combined rather than as separately normalized Z0 and Z0' source estimates.
do_not_use_for: Do not split this into standalone Z0 and Z0' residual theorems unless the source text explicitly supports that split.
Do not claim this entry alone proves the repository source-to-Lean dictionaries for D, P, Z0, or Z0'.
Do not collapse the following final Z0' summation/majorization into Eq. (2.37) without recording it as a separate source step.
open_questions: Exact source-to-Lean dictionary for the D, P, Z0, and Z0' indices in Eq. (2.37).
Exact formal theorem deriving CMP116Eq237MajorizationBoundary from the visual Eq. (2.37), alpha5, C3, and metric-comparison constants.
Exact identities or common majorants for the O(1) constants in the Eq. (2.37) and post-Eq. (2.37) paragraphs.

citations.summary: Geometric bound relating the connected-component distances inside Z0 to the gap size |Z0 \ Y0| and d_k(Z0).
extracted_claims: After the Eq. (2.31) P-sum, Balaban writes Z0 as a union of connected components and lets one component Z0 contain Y0 = union_i Y_i.
Eq. (2.32) visually gives sum_i d_k(Y_i) + 4*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0| >= d_k(Z0).
The post-Eq. (2.37) paragraph later says this inequality is used in an adapted form for the final Z0' or Z \ Z0' summation.
This entry records the geometric source anchor only; it does not prove the adapted Z0' version or any source-to-Lean index dictionary.
use_for: Source anchor for the geometric distance tradeoff referenced in the final post-(2.37) summation.
Source anchor for a future theorem proving the adapted Eq. (2.32) bound used after Eq. (2.37).
do_not_use_for: Do not cite the page-18 Eq. (2.32) display alone as proving the post-(2.37) adapted Z0' summation.
Do not use this entry to discharge hpost_eq237 without a formal adapted-index theorem.
Do not infer the Eq. (2.31) P-family carrier or membership iff from this geometric inequality.
open_questions: Exact adapted Eq. (2.32) theorem for the post-(2.37) Z0' situation.
Source-to-Lean meaning of the sets Z0, Y0, Z0', and Z \ Z0' in the adapted use.
How the four-gap term 4*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0| becomes the Lean residual exponent budget.

citations.summary: Finite summation over Y0 subsets of Z0, or equivalently Z0 \ Y0, used before and by analogy after Eq. (2.37).
extracted_claims: Balaban reduces the last pre-(2.35) sum to a sum over Y0 subset Z0, or over Z0 \ Y0.
Eq. (2.34) bounds the sum of exp(-constant*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0|) terms by an exponential depending on M^-4*|Z0|.
The paragraph after Eq. (2.37) explicitly invokes an inequality similar to Eq. (2.34) for the sum over Z \ Z0'.
This entry records the source summation pattern only; it does not identify the post-(2.37) source Z0' family or prove the adapted final summation.
use_for: Source anchor for the Eq. (2.34)-type finite subset summation referenced after Eq. (2.37).
Source anchor for a future hpost_eq237 proof after the post-(2.37) source family is identified.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat Eq. (2.34) itself as the final post-(2.37) summation theorem.
Do not collapse its scalar constant into the Eq. (2.37) O(1) constants without a majorant theorem.
Do not use this entry to assert a standalone normalized Z0' residual theorem.
open_questions: Exact adapted Eq. (2.34)-type theorem over Z \ Z0' in the post-(2.37) paragraph.
Exact scalar constant and lower-bound hypothesis needed for the remaining half of the first exponential factor.
Source-to-Lean dictionary for the final sourceZ0PrimeIndex family.

citations.summary: Scale-transfer geometric inequality used when bounding the Z0 sum with fixed Z0'.
extracted_claims: Before Eq. (2.37), Balaban fixes Z0' and decomposes the set Z0 into connected components Z_i determining connected components Z_i' of Z0'.
Eq. (2.36) visually gives 2*d_k(Z_i) >= L*d_{k+1}(Z_i').
Balaban states that this follows from simple but awkward geometric and combinatorial considerations by checking locally many cases.
This entry records the scale-transfer source anchor only; it does not prove the local-case combinatorial theorem in Lean.
use_for: Source anchor for the component-metric comparison in the fixed-Z0' Eq. (2.37) estimate.
Source anchor for a future Lean theorem deriving the seven-delta component decay in cmp116Eq237FixedZ0PrimeWeight.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite Eq. (2.36) as already proving the full fixed-Z0' source estimate.
Do not omit the required local combinatorial proof if this inequality is theorem-fed into Lean.
Do not use this entry to identify the repository components with Balaban's Z_i or Z_i' without a dictionary theorem.
open_questions: Formal source-to-Lean definitions of Z_i, Z_i', and the component metrics d_k and d_{k+1}.
Lean proof or source-backed finite local-case theorem for 2*d_k(Z_i) >= L*d_{k+1}(Z_i').
Exact connection between this metric comparison and the (1 - 7*delta)/2 exponent in Eq. (2.37).

citations.summary: Definition region for alpha5 restrictions and the final C3 constant used in Lemma 3.
extracted_claims: After Eq. (2.37), Balaban recalls alpha5 as alpha5 = O(1)*exp(-(1/3)*delta0*M) + O(alpha0 + alpha1) + O(1)*alpha4 + gamma2.
The source assumes (LM)^4*alpha0, (LM)^4*alpha1, (LM)^4*alpha4, and (LM)^4*gamma2 are bounded by an M-independent constant, for example by 1.
Under those bounds, O(1)*(LM)^4*alpha5 + exp(-(1/2)*(kappa1 - 1)) is bounded by an absolute constant.

Balaban then uses the factor exp(-(delta/2)*L*kappa*d_{k+1}(Z)) and Eq. (2.30) to bound the remaining exponentials by 1, leaves one factor 2*(L+2)^4*O(1)*epsilon2, and estimates the remaining factors by 1.

The visual definition of the final Lemma-3 constant is C3 = 2*(L+2)^4*O(1)*2*E0*C1*alpha4^-1*alpha6^-1*M^q*exp(C2*kappa1), followed by Lemma 3's |H(Z)| <= C3*epsilon1*exp(-((1 - 8*delta)/2)*L*kappa*d_{k+1}(Z)).
use_for: Source anchor for the alpha5 definition region, the boundedness assumptions on (LM)^4 times alpha0/alpha1/alpha4/gamma2, and the visual C3 formula.
Source anchor for the final Lemma-3 amplitude shape C3*epsilon1 in Eq. (2.38).
do_not_use_for: Do not collapse repeated O(1) constants into one Lean constant without a source majorant theorem.
Do not infer exact numeric values for the O(1), E0, C1, C2, or q constants from this visual entry alone.
Do not use the final Lemma 3 bound to backfill the upstream Eq. (2.31) P-family carrier or Eq. (2.29) D-family dictionary.
open_questions: Exact earlier definitions of epsilon1, epsilon2, gamma2, alpha0, alpha1, alpha4, alpha6, E0, C1, C2, and q.
Whether distinct O(1) constants need distinct Lean fields or a common majorant.
Exact source theorem or constant-hierarchy package proving the displayed boundedness and smallness assumptions uniformly in scale.

citations.summary: Final Lemma 3 activity estimate for H(Z), after all restrictions and resummations.
use_for: Final target shape of the Lemma 3 activity estimate.
do_not_use_for: Do not use (2.38) to pretend the upstream Eq. (2.31) or post-P source boundaries have been proved separately.
open_questions: Exact exponent convention: OCR and existing notes differ on visible half-power/one-half formatting.
Full restriction list preceding Lemma 3.

citations.summary: Post-Lemma-3 insertion of the H(Z) bound into the effective-action estimate.
use_for: Downstream effective-action source anchor after Lemma 3 is available.
do_not_use_for: Do not use this as a proof of the Lemma 3 resummation itself.
open_questions: Exact Lean theorem target for the post-Lemma-3 effective-action consumer.

citations.summary: Localized R-term decay in the convergent expansion paper.
use_for: Later R-operation/localized remainder source targeting.
do_not_use_for: Do not substitute this CMP119 R-term bound for CMP116 Lemma 3's H(Z) estimate.
